name: Brent Crude
summary: Day 65. BRENT $108.17 (-5.1% 1D, +6.9% 1M, +60.7% 3M) — DOWN $9-12/BBL FROM APR 29 HIGHS IN 2 SESSIONS ON IRAN 14-POINT PROPOSAL. The $115-sustained-2-session trigger RESOLVED-NO (Apr 29 $118-120, Apr 30 ~$110.40, May 1 $108.17). Proximate driver: Iran 14-point proposal delivered via Pakistan May 1 (CNBC primary attribution). Cross-asset: WTI $101.94 -3%, RBOB -4.7%, Heating Oil -4.6%, Urea -14.23%, Iran rial decelerated, VIX -10%, S&P record. Operational reality unchanged: blockade in place, Iran exports 567K bpd (-73%), $500M/day revenue loss, 26-76 day storage runway. DIPLOMATIC RE-PRICING move, not SUPPLY DISRUPTION REVERSAL. If Trump publicly engages within 7 days, Brent likely tests $100-105; if rejects, rebounds to $112-118 within 5 sessions. UAE OPEC exit effective May 1 was calendar-priced through April. Deployment-signal counter-trigger 'Brent <$115 sustained 5+ sessions' Day 2 confirmed.
